heading: What is Technical Debt?
body: Technical debt is exactly like financial debt. When you take a shortcut in your code -- skipping tests, copying and pasting instead of abstracting, hardcoding values -- you are borrowing time from your future self. And just like a credit card, that debt accumulates interest. The longer you wait to pay it off, the more expensive it becomes. A quick hack that saves you 30 minutes today can cost your team 30 hours six months from now when nobody remembers why the code works that way.
